title: 11 Herbal Teas For Taste Changes
intro_desc: Herbal teas have been used for centuries to help with taste changes, and it's not just a myth. When you're feeling down or recovering from an illness, your sense of taste can be affected, making food and drinks taste bland or metallic. This is where herbal teas come in - they can not only soothe your senses but also provide a boost to your taste buds.  For example, Zingiber officinale, commonly known as ginger, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to a change in taste. Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to heal and protect the mucous membranes in your mouth and throat. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has been used for centuries to soothe stomach issues and has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce irritation in the mouth and throat.  Drinking herbal teas like these can help to stimulate your taste buds and get your sense of taste back on track. When you drink herbal tea, the flavors and aromas can help to stimulate the nerve endings in your mouth and throat, which can help to increase saliva production and reduce inflammation. This can help to restore your sense of taste and make food and drinks taste more flavorful again. Plus, the antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in these herbs can help to boost your immune system and reduce inflammation in your body, which can help to prevent future taste changes.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent taste changes from forming. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, have a strong flavor that can cover up other tastes. They can also help stimulate the taste buds. Regularly drinking herbal tea, especially after chemotherapy or certain medications, may help keep the sense of taste from changing.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea might help prevent taste changes from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, have strong flavors that can help keep your taste buds active. This can be especially helpful if you're undergoing chemotherapy or radiation treatment, which can cause a decrease in taste.
faq_safe: You can safely drink herbal teas with changing tastes every day, but be aware of potential interactions. Some herbs can affect medications or worsen health conditions. If you have a health issue, monitor your body's reaction. Common herbs like peppermint and chamomile are generally safe, but it's essential to be cautious and informed.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can start to change the way your taste buds work within a few days to a week. Some people notice a difference in their taste sensitivity right away, while others take a bit longer. This is because it takes some time for your taste buds to adjust and respond to the herbs.
faq_time: Herbal tea tastes best at different times of day. If you drink it in the morning, you might notice the flavors of fruits and flowers. In the afternoon, earthy and herbal notes come out. At night, spices and calming effects shine through. Experiment to find when your favorite herbal tea tastes the best to you.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can change how your body uses some prescription medications. For example, certain herbs like ginkgo and St. John's Wort can affect how well your body breaks down certain medications. This might lead to unexpected side effects or changes in how a medication tastes. Some people find their prescription medicine tastes different after drinking herbal teas.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's generally a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs can affect the baby's development or cause problems during pregnancy. If you're drinking herbal teas for taste changes, try to stick to ones that are considered safe, like peppermint or ginger. However, it's best to avoid any that might be a risk.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a great way to change up the flavor of your drinks while breastfeeding. However, it's best to check the ingredients first. Some herbs can pass into your milk and affect your baby. Caffeine and certain spices, like pennyroyal and sage, are among those to watch out for.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea. The gingerols and shogaols in Zingiber officinale teas have been shown to stimulate digestion and improve appetite, making it beneficial for people experiencing taste changes due to digestive problems. The antioxidant properties of these compounds also help to protect the stomach and intestines from damage, promoting a healthy gut and reducing inflammation. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy digestion, Zingiber officinale teas can help alleviate taste changes caused by digestive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Zingiber officinale (ginger) roots.
Peel and chop the ginger roots into small pieces. Use 1-2 tablespoons for a single serving.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot and add the chopped ginger pieces.
Steep the ginger in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup and serve hot or with honey to taste.

remedy_amazon.title: FGO Organic Ginger Tea, 100 Count, Eco-Conscious Tea Bags, Caffeine Free, Packaging May Vary (Pack of 1)

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa teas contains bioactive constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation that may be contributing to taste changes. Curcumin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which can help alleviate symptoms of taste disorders. The antioxidants in Curcuma longa teas may also help repair and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, which can become damaged and affect taste. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Curcuma longa teas may help restore normal taste function and alleviate taste changes.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric roots (Curcuma longa) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the turmeric to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of honey or sugar to taste, if desired.
Serve the turmeric tea warm and enjoy the potential health benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains licorice root extract, which is rich in compounds like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Glycyrrhizin helps to balance the flavor and aroma of food by reducing bitterness and increasing sweetness. Flavonoids like isoliquertigenin and licoricidin have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe and calm the digestive system. The phenolic acids present in licorice root tea may also help to reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing inflammation, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can help alleviate changes in taste perception caused by digestive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Boil the water in a pot and add the dried root.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the root. Let the tea cool down.
Serve the tea in a cup and adjust the taste with honey or lemon to your liking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas contains rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and borneol as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can cause taste changes. The antioxidant properties of rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid can also help to protect the mucous membranes from damage and promote healing. Additionally, borneol's antimicrobial properties can help to reduce the presence of bacteria and other pathogens that can cause infections and disrupt taste. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Rosmarinus officinalis teas may help to alleviate taste changes and restore normal taste function.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey or sugar to taste if desired, then mix well.
Enjoy your Rosmarinus officinalis tea hot or cold, sweet or unsweet.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas contains active constituents like cinnamaldehyde, cinnamyl acetate, and linalool, which help with taste changes. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing irritation and discomfort. The antioxidant properties of linalool and cinnamaldehyde also protect the tongue and taste buds from damage, allowing them to function properly. Additionally, cinnamaldehyde has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of bacteria and yeast in the mouth, which can contribute to unpleasant tastes. By reducing inflammation and controlling microorganisms, Cinnamomum verum teas helps to restore a normal sense of taste and smell.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Cinnamomum verum bark, 1 cup of boiling water, and a cup.
Add 1 teaspoon of sugar (optional) to the cup.
Place the Cinnamomum verum bark in the cup and pour in the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Add more sugar if needed and enjoy your Cinnamomum verum tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide. These compounds help protect the tongue's taste buds from oxidative stress, which can damage them and lead to changes in taste. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the mouth and improve taste perception. The terpenoids, including ginkgolides, can help increase blood flow to the tongue, which may improve taste sensitivity. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, and improving blood flow, Ginkgo biloba teas may help alleviate taste changes.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Pour the boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser or muslin bag. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and enjoy. You can add honey or lemon to taste.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum teas contains silymarin, a mixture of flavonoids, including silibinin, silidianin, and silicristin. These compounds have antioxidant properties that can help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract from damage. Silymarin also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth, helping to alleviate symptoms of taste changes. Additionally, silymarin has been shown to improve liver function, which is essential for detoxifying the body and maintaining a healthy taste sense. By supporting liver health and reducing inflammation, Silybum marianum teas can help restore a normal sense of taste.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Silybum marianum leaves and flowers.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey to 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1/2 cup of the fresh Silybum marianum leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture into another cup.
Add the honey mixture to the strained tea and enjoy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat. These properties can reduce irritation and inflammation that can cause changes in taste. The tea also contains flavonoids like kaempferol, which can help repair and regenerate damaged cells, contributing to a healthy oral environment. The antioxidants in Echinacea purpurea tea can also neutralize free radicals that can damage taste buds and affect taste perception. By addressing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment, Echinacea purpurea tea may be beneficial in supporting taste changes.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a tea kettle.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Echinacea purpurea and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and enjoy it hot or cold with honey and lemon to taste.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis teas contains rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin, which are bioactive compounds that contribute to its benefits. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe digestive issues and alleviate taste changes. Melissa officinalis teas also contains flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat. The antimicrobial properties of Melissa officinalis teas can also help combat infections that may contribute to taste changes. By reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membranes, Melissa officinalis teas may help restore a normal sense of taste and smell.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Melissa officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Melissa officinalis leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or tea infuser.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea immediately, as the flavor and aroma will fade over time.

remedies.remedy_desc: Origanum vulgare teas contains the bioactive constituents carvacrol, thymol, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the mouth and throat, a common cause of taste changes. The antioxidant properties of rosmarinic acid can also protect the tongue's taste buds from damage and oxidative stress. Additionally, carvacrol has antimicrobial properties, which can help control infections that may be contributing to taste changes. By reducing inflammation, protecting taste buds, and controlling infections, Origanum vulgare teas may help alleviate taste changes.
remedies.image_alt: Origanum vulgare herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Origanum vulgare leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Origanum vulgare leaves per 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Origanum vulgare leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas contains bioactive constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues often associated with taste changes. The relaxing effects of linalool also calm the nervous system, which can contribute to appetite loss and other taste-related problems. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of linalyl acetate protect the stomach lining, reducing irritation and inflammation that can disrupt taste perception. By addressing these underlying issues, Lavandula angustifolia teas may help alleviate taste changes caused by digestive problems.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for taste changes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and place it in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Heat 1 cup of water to 100°F (38°C) to 212°F (100°C).
Steep the Lavandula angustifolia flowers in the hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and serve it hot or cold, according to your preference.
